content:
One practical SEO check I use on small business/client sites is making sure the page sends the same indexability message everywhere.
For any important page, check:

• HTTP status is 200, not a soft 404 or redirect chain

• Meta robots does not say noindex

• Canonical points to itself or to the exact intended URL

• The page is present in the XML sitemap only if it should be indexed

• Internal links point to the canonical URL, not a parameterized or trailing-slash variant
The mistake I see a lot: a page is in the sitemap, internally linked, and meant to rank, but the canonical points somewhere else because of a CMS template, duplicate landing page, or staging-to-prod migration issue.
Before changing copy or building more pages, crawl the site and compare these four columns: status code, indexability, canonical target, and sitemap inclusion. If they disagree, fix that first. It is boring, but it prevents a lot of “why won’t Google pick this up?” guessing.

  submitted by   /u/VRTCLS
[link]   [comments]
